notes: Round 2 re-reviewed the follow-up fix for the prior `row_id` blocker. The backend compatibility error is resolved, but the detail content pane still loads by plain `skillId`, so duplicate Claude detail is still not fully source-aware.
The remaining blocker now sits in the fix feature: metadata requests are row-aware, but SKILL.md content is still fetched through `read_skill_content({ skillId })` instead of the selected row path.

blockingIssues.description: `src/stores/skillDetailStore.ts:187` still loads content with `read_skill_content({ skillId })` after fetching row-aware detail. Because `src-tauri/src/commands/skills.rs:357-366` resolves content through `db::get_skill_by_id`, duplicate Claude rows still collapse onto the manageable/canonical file, and marketplace-only survivors can still fail content loading. The selected detail row must instead load content from its resolved path (for example via `read_file_by_path(detail.file_path)`) to preserve source-aware detail behavior.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For fix follow-ups and reruns, require the worker to reread any mission library notes added after the original failure when those notes document the exact API or contract they are repairing.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `claude-row-id-detail-compatibility-fix` found that `.factory/library/claude-source-aware-detail-api.md` already documented the required `read_file_by_path(detail.file_path)` follow-up, but the frontend worker skill only mandated a fixed startup reading list and the fix still used `read_skill_content({ skillId })`.

rejectedObservations.observation: The backend worker skill/procedure lacks frontend-targeted validation guidance for IPC payload changes.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-addressed: `.factory/skills/claude-scan-backend-worker/SKILL.md` now explicitly requires `test-frontend-targeted`, `typecheck`, and `lint` when frontend-consumed payloads change.

rejectedObservations.observation: The backend worker skill/procedure lacks guidance for producing a scoped feature commit when the shared mission workspace is already dirty from unrelated work.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in mission AGENTS.md, which already tells workers not to clean/stash unrelated dirty files and to commit only feature-owned files.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker marked `followedProcedure` true even though the reviewed feature stored `row_id` internally without exposing it on the reviewed API surface.
rejectedObservations.reason: feature-specific reviewer feedback rather than a durable shared-state rule or repository knowledge update.
